# MarkdownPastePlugin Implementation Plan

## Context

The `@haklex/rich-editor` currently handles paste events only for images (via `ImageUploadPlugin`). When users paste Markdown text (e.g., from GitHub, documentation, or VS Code), it's inserted as plain text. This plugin auto-detects pasted Markdown and converts it to rich Lexical nodes, and also handles VS Code paste as code blocks.

## Architecture

### New File

- `haklex/rich-editor/src/plugins/MarkdownPastePlugin.tsx`

### Modified Files

- `haklex/rich-editor/src/components/RichEditor.tsx` — mount `<MarkdownPastePlugin />`
- `haklex/rich-editor/src/plugins/index.ts` — export plugin

## Plugin Design

Register `PASTE_COMMAND` at `COMMAND_PRIORITY_HIGH`. The handler follows this decision tree:

```
PASTE_COMMAND received
├─ Has `vscode-editor-data` type?
│  └─ YES → Create CodeBlock node with language + code → return true
├─ Has `text/html` with real formatting?
│  └─ YES → return false (let Lexical default HTML paste handle)
├─ Get `text/plain`, run markdown detection
│  ├─ score >= threshold → convert markdown → insert nodes → return true
│  └─ score < threshold → return false (plain text paste)
└─ No text → return false
```

### Step 1: VS Code Paste Detection

```typescript
function getVSCodePasteData(clipboardData: DataTransfer): { language: string } | null {
  const raw = clipboardData.getData('vscode-editor-data')
  if (!raw) return null
  try {
    const data = JSON.parse(raw)
    return { language: data.mode || 'text' }
  } catch {
    return null
  }
}
```

When detected, split by double blank lines (`\n\n\n+`) into separate code blocks:

```typescript
const code = clipboardData.getData('text/plain')
const segments = code.split(/\n{3,}/).filter(Boolean)
const nodes = segments.map((seg) => $createCodeBlockEditNode(seg.trim(), language))
$insertNodes(nodes)
```

Use `$createCodeBlockEditNode` (not `$createCodeBlockNode`) since we're in the editor context. Import from `../nodes/CodeBlockEditNode`. Single blank lines within code are preserved — only 2+ consecutive blank lines trigger splitting.

### Step 2: Rich HTML Check

```typescript
function hasRichHTML(clipboardData: DataTransfer): boolean {
  const html = clipboardData.getData('text/html')
  if (!html) return false
  // VS Code HTML contains data-vscode attributes — not "rich"
  if (/data-vscode|vscode-/i.test(html)) return false
  // Check for formatting tags beyond bare <p>/<span>
  const doc = new DOMParser().parseFromString(html, 'text/html')
  const richTags = doc.body.querySelectorAll(
    'strong,em,b,i,h1,h2,h3,h4,h5,h6,ul,ol,table,img,blockquote,pre>code,a[href]'
  )
  return richTags.length > 0
}
```

If `hasRichHTML` → `return false` to let Lexical's built-in HTML→Lexical conversion handle it.

### Step 3: Markdown Detection (Scoring)

```typescript
function detectMarkdown(text: string): boolean {
  let score = 0

  // High confidence (5 pts each)
  if (/^#{1,6}\s+\S/m.test(text)) score += 5          // ATX heading
  if (/^```[\w-]*$/m.test(text)) score += 5            // Fenced code block
  if (/\[.+?\]\(.+?\)/.test(text)) score += 4          // Link
  if (/!\[.*?\]\(.+?\)/.test(text)) score += 5         // Image
  if (/^\|.+\|$/m.test(text) &&
      /^\|[-:\s|]+\|$/m.test(text)) score += 5         // Table
  if (/^>\s*\[!(NOTE|TIP|WARNING|CAUTION|IMPORTANT)\]/mi.test(text)) score += 5
  if (/^[-*]\s+\[[ x]\]/m.test(text)) score += 4      // Task list

  // Medium confidence (2 pts each)
  if (/\*\*.+?\*\*/.test(text)) score += 2             // Bold
  if (/(?<!\*)\*(?!\*)(?!\s).+?(?<!\s)(?<!\*)\*(?!\*)/.test(text)) score += 1
  if (/^[-*+]\s+\S/m.test(text)) score += 1            // Unordered list
  if (/^\d+\.\s+\S/m.test(text)) score += 1            // Ordered list
  if (/^>\s+\S/m.test(text)) score += 1                // Blockquote
  if (/`.+?`/.test(text)) score += 1                    // Inline code
  if (/^[-*_]{3,}$/m.test(text)) score += 2            // Horizontal rule

  // Structural signals
  const paragraphs = text.split(/\n{2,}/).filter(Boolean)
  if (paragraphs.length >= 3) score += 2               // Multi-paragraph

  // Negative signals
  if (text.length < 20) score -= 3
  if (!text.includes('\n')) score -= 2

  return score >= 5
}
```

### Step 4: Markdown → Lexical Node Conversion

Use a temp editor to convert markdown, then serialize → deserialize into the real editor:

```typescript
import { createEditor, $parseSerializedNode, $insertNodes } from 'lexical'
import { $convertFromMarkdownString } from '@lexical/markdown'
import { getResolvedEditNodes } from '../node-registry'
import { ALL_TRANSFORMERS } from '../transformers'

function convertAndInsert(editor: LexicalEditor, markdown: string): void {
  // Create temp editor with same node types
  const tempEditor = createEditor({
    namespace: 'markdown-paste-temp',
    nodes: getResolvedEditNodes(),
    onError: () => {},
  })

  // Synchronous conversion in temp editor
  tempEditor.update(
    () => { $convertFromMarkdownString(markdown, ALL_TRANSFORMERS) },
    { discrete: true },
  )

  // Serialize nodes from temp editor
  const serializedChildren = tempEditor.getEditorState().toJSON().root.children

  // Insert into real editor (already in editor.update context via PASTE_COMMAND)
  const nodes = serializedChildren.map((s) => $parseSerializedNode(s))
  $insertNodes(nodes)
}
```

Key: `{ discrete: true }` makes `tempEditor.update()` synchronous. `$parseSerializedNode` runs in the real editor's context, so nodes are properly registered.

### Step 5: Plugin Component

```typescript
export function MarkdownPastePlugin() {
  const [editor] = useLexicalComposerContext()

  useEffect(() => {
    return editor.registerCommand(
      PASTE_COMMAND,
      (event) => {
        const clipboardData = 'clipboardData' in event
          ? (event as ClipboardEvent).clipboardData
          : null
        if (!clipboardData) return false

        // 1. VS Code paste → code block(s), split by 2+ blank lines
        const vscodeData = getVSCodePasteData(clipboardData)
        if (vscodeData) {
          const code = clipboardData.getData('text/plain')
          if (code) {
            const segments = code.split(/\n{3,}/).filter(Boolean)
            const nodes = segments.map((s) =>
              $createCodeBlockEditNode(s.trim(), vscodeData.language)
            )
            $insertNodes(nodes)
            return true
          }
        }

        // 2. Rich HTML → let Lexical handle
        if (hasRichHTML(clipboardData)) return false

        // 3. Markdown detection
        const text = clipboardData.getData('text/plain')
        if (!text || !detectMarkdown(text)) return false

        // 4. Convert and insert
        event.preventDefault()
        convertAndInsert(editor, text)
        return true
      },
      COMMAND_PRIORITY_HIGH,
    )
  }, [editor])

  return null
}
```

### Step 6: Mount in RichEditor

In `RichEditor.tsx`, add after `MarkdownShortcutsPlugin`:

```tsx
<MarkdownPastePlugin />
```

### Step 7: Export from plugins/index.ts

```typescript
export { MarkdownPastePlugin } from './MarkdownPastePlugin'
```

## Edge Cases Handled

| Case | Behavior |
|------|----------|
| VS Code paste | `vscode-editor-data` type → CodeBlock(s) with language, split by 2+ blank lines |
| Rich HTML (browser copy) | Skip, let Lexical HTML paste handle |
| VS Code HTML (syntax spans) | `data-vscode` marker → skip HTML, fall through to VS Code handler |
| Markdown text | Score >= 5 → convert via `$convertFromMarkdownString` |
| Plain text (not markdown) | Score < 5 → default paste |
| Short text / single line | Negative scoring → unlikely to trigger |
| Multi-paragraph markdown | `\n\n` separation adds +2 to score |
| Undo | Lexical `HistoryPlugin` handles automatically |
| Paste into middle of content | `$insertNodes` handles cursor-position insertion |
| Empty editor paste | Same flow — `$insertNodes` works on empty root |

## Verification

1. `pnpm --filter @haklex/rich-editor-demo dev` — open dev playground
2. Paste markdown text (e.g., a GitHub README) → should render as rich nodes
3. Paste from VS Code → should create code block with correct language
4. Paste plain text (no markdown syntax) → should insert as plain text
5. Paste rich HTML from browser → should use Lexical's HTML paste (not markdown)
6. Ctrl+Z after markdown paste → should revert to state before paste
7. Paste markdown into middle of existing content → should insert at cursor

    78→  return {
    79→    src: await readAsDataUrl(file),
    80→    altText: file.name,
    81→  }
    82→}
    83→
    84→interface ImageUploadPluginProps {
    85→  onUpload: ImageUploadFn
    86→}
    87→
    88→export function ImageUploadPlugin({ onUpload }: ImageUploadPluginProps) {
    89→  const [editor] = useLexicalComposerContext()
    90→  const uploadRef = useRef(onUpload)
    91→  uploadRef.current = onUpload
    92→  const fileInputRef = useRef<HTMLInputElement>(null)
    93→  const toastTimerRef = useRef<number | null>(null)
    94→
    95→  const [dialogOpen, setDialogOpen] = useState(false)
    96→  const [tab, setTab] = useState<'upload' | 'url'>('upload')
    97→  const [rootDragActive, setRootDragActive] = useState(false)
    98→  const [dialogDragActive, setDialogDragActive] = useState(false)
    99→  const [pendingUploads, setPendingUploads] = useState(0)
   100→  const [dialogUploading, setDialogUploading] = useState(false)
   101→  const [toast, setToast] = useState<null | {
   102→    kind: 'success' | 'error'
   103→    message: string
   104→  }>(null)
   105→
   106→  const [urlInput, setUrlInput] = useState('')
   107→  const [urlPreview, setUrlPreview] = useState<string | null>(null)
   108→  const [urlMeta, setUrlMeta] = useState<{
   109→    width: number
   110→    height: number
   111→  } | null>(null)
   112→  const [urlLoading, setUrlLoading] = useState(false)
   113→  const [urlError, setUrlError] = useState<string | null>(null)
   114→
   115→  const tabItems = useMemo(
   116→    () => [
   117→      { value: 'upload' as const, label: 'Upload' },
   118→      { value: 'url' as const, label: 'URL' },
   119→    ],
   120→    [],
   121→  )
   122→
   123→  const pushToast = useCallback(
   124→    (kind: 'success' | 'error', message: string) => {
   125→      setToast({ kind, message })
   126→      if (toastTimerRef.current) {
   127→        window.clearTimeout(toastTimerRef.current)
   128→      }
   129→      toastTimerRef.current = window.setTimeout(() => setToast(null), 2200)
   130→    },
   131→    [],
   132→  )
   133→
   134→  const insertByUpload = useCallback(
   135→    async (file: File, options?: { closeDialog?: boolean }) => {
   136→      if (!isImageFile(file)) return false
   137→
   138→      const closeDialog = Boolean(options?.closeDialog)
   139→
   140→      setPendingUploads((value) => value + 1)
   141→      if (closeDialog) setDialogUploading(true)
   142→
   143→      try {
   144→        const [result, meta] = await Promise.all([
   145→          uploadRef.current(file),
   146→          computeImageMeta(file),
   147→        ])
   148→
   149→        editor.update(() => {
   150→          const node = $createImageNode({
   151→            src: result.src,
   152→            altText: result.altText ?? file.name,
   153→            width: result.width ?? meta.width,
   154→            height: result.height ?? meta.height,
   155→            thumbhash: result.thumbhash ?? meta.thumbhash,
   156→          })
   157→          $insertNodes([node])
   158→        })
   159→
   160→        if (closeDialog) {
   161→          setDialogOpen(false)
   162→          setUrlInput('')
   163→          setUrlPreview(null)
   164→          setUrlMeta(null)
   165→          setUrlError(null)
   166→        }
   167→        pushToast('success', 'Image uploaded')
   168→        return true
   169→      } catch (err: unknown) {
   170→        console.error('[ImageUploadPlugin]', err)
   171→        pushToast('error', 'Image upload failed')
   172→        return false
   173→      } finally {
   174→        setPendingUploads((value) => Math.max(value - 1, 0))
   175→        setDialogUploading(false)
   176→      }
   177→    },
   178→    [editor, pushToast],
   179→  )
   180→
   181→  const handleFiles = useCallback(
   182→    (files: File[]): boolean => {
   183→      const images = files.filter(isImageFile)
   184→      if (images.length === 0) return false
   185→
   186→      for (const file of images) {
   187→        void insertByUpload(file)
   188→      }
   189→      return true
   190→    },
   191→    [insertByUpload],
   192→  )
   193→
   194→  useEffect(() => {
   195→    const unregisterDragDrop = editor.registerCommand(
   196→      DRAG_DROP_PASTE,
   197→      (files: File[]) => handleFiles(files),
   198→      COMMAND_PRIORITY_HIGH,
   199→    )
   200→
   201→    const unregisterPaste = editor.registerCommand(
   202→      PASTE_COMMAND,
   203→      (event: ClipboardEvent | InputEvent | KeyboardEvent) => {
   204→        const clipboardData =
   205→          'clipboardData' in event
   206→            ? (event as ClipboardEvent).clipboardData
   207→            : null
   208→        if (!clipboardData) return false
   209→
   210→        const files = Array.from(clipboardData.files)
   211→        if (files.some(isImageFile)) {
   212→          return handleFiles(files)
   213→        }
   214→        return false
   215→      },
   216→      COMMAND_PRIORITY_HIGH,
   217→    )
   218→
   219→    const unregisterOpenDialog = editor.registerCommand(
   220→      OPEN_IMAGE_UPLOAD_DIALOG_COMMAND,
   221→      () => {
   222→        setDialogOpen(true)
   223→        return true
   224→      },
   225→      COMMAND_PRIORITY_EDITOR,
   226→    )
   227→
   228→    const rootElement = editor.getRootElement()
   229→    const wrapper = rootElement?.parentElement ?? null
   230→    if (!wrapper) {
   231→      return () => {
   232→        unregisterDragDrop()
   233→        unregisterPaste()
   234→        unregisterOpenDialog()
   235→      }
   236→    }
   237→
   238→    let dragCounter = 0
   239→
   240→    const setWrapperDragging = (next: boolean) => {
   241→      setRootDragActive(next)
   242→      wrapper.classList.toggle(css.draggingWrapperClass, next)
   243→    }
   244→
   245→    const onDragEnter = (event: DragEvent) => {
   246→      if (!hasImageData(event.dataTransfer)) return
   247→      dragCounter += 1
   248→      setWrapperDragging(true)
   249→    }
   250→
   251→    const onDragOver = (event: DragEvent) => {
   252→      if (!hasImageData(event.dataTransfer)) return
   253→      event.preventDefault()
   254→    }
   255→
   256→    const onDragLeave = () => {
   257→      dragCounter = Math.max(dragCounter - 1, 0)
   258→      if (dragCounter === 0) {
   259→        setWrapperDragging(false)
   260→      }
   261→    }
   262→
   263→    const onDrop = () => {
   264→      dragCounter = 0
   265→      setWrapperDragging(false)
   266→    }
   267→
   268→    rootElement?.addEventListener('dragenter', onDragEnter)
   269→    rootElement?.addEventListener('dragover', onDragOver)
   270→    rootElement?.addEventListener('dragleave', onDragLeave)
   271→    rootElement?.addEventListener('drop', onDrop)
   272→
   273→    return () => {
   274→      if (toastTimerRef.current) {
   275→        window.clearTimeout(toastTimerRef.current)
   276→      }
   277→      unregisterDragDrop()
   278→      unregisterPaste()
   279→      unregisterOpenDialog()
   280→      setWrapperDragging(false)
   281→      rootElement?.removeEventListener('dragenter', onDragEnter)
   282→      rootElement?.removeEventListener('dragover', onDragOver)
   283→      rootElement?.removeEventListener('dragleave', onDragLeave)
   284→      rootElement?.removeEventListener('drop', onDrop)
   285→    }
   286→  }, [editor, handleFiles])
   287→
   288→  const resetUrlState = useCallback(() => {
   289→    setUrlInput('')
   290→    setUrlPreview(null)
   291→    setUrlMeta(null)
   292→    setUrlError(null)
   293→    setUrlLoading(false)
   294→  }, [])
   295→
   296→  const handleDialogFile = useCallback(
   297→    async (file: File | null) => {
   298→      if (!file) return
   299→      await insertByUpload(file, { closeDialog: true })
   300→    },
   301→    [insertByUpload],
   302→  )
   303→
   304→  const handleUrlPreview = useCallback(async () => {
   305→    const nextUrl = urlInput.trim()
   306→    if (!nextUrl) return
   307→    if (!isSafeImageUrl(nextUrl)) {
   308→      setUrlError('Unsupported URL scheme')
   309→      return
   310→    }
   311→
   312→    setUrlLoading(true)
   313→    setUrlError(null)
   314→    try {
   315→      const meta = await loadImageByUrl(nextUrl)
   316→      setUrlMeta(meta)
   317→      setUrlPreview(nextUrl)
   318→    } catch {
   319→      setUrlPreview(null)
   320→      setUrlMeta(null)
   321→      setUrlError('Could not load this image URL')
   322→    } finally {
   323→      setUrlLoading(false)
   324→    }
   325→  }, [urlInput])
   326→
   327→  const handleInsertByUrl = useCallback(() => {
   328→    if (!urlPreview || !isSafeImageUrl(urlPreview)) return
   329→
   330→    editor.update(() => {
   331→      const node = $createImageNode({
   332→        src: urlPreview,
   333→        altText: '',
   334→        width: urlMeta?.width,
   335→        height: urlMeta?.height,
   336→      })
   337→      $insertNodes([node])
   338→    })
   339→    pushToast('success', 'Image inserted')
   340→    setDialogOpen(false)
   341→    resetUrlState()
   342→  }, [editor, pushToast, resetUrlState, urlMeta, urlPreview])
   343→
   344→  const helperMessage =
   345→    pendingUploads > 0
   346→      ? `Uploading ${pendingUploads} image${pendingUploads > 1 ? 's' : ''}...`
   347→      : rootDragActive
   348→        ? 'Drop image files to upload'
   349→        : null
   350→
   351→  return (
   352→    <>
   353→      {(helperMessage || toast) && (
   354→        <div className={css.toastStack}>
   355→          {helperMessage && (
   356→            <div className={`${css.toast} ${css.toastVariant.info}`}>
   357→              <span className={css.spinner} />
   358→              {helperMessage}
   359→            </div>
   360→          )}
   361→          {toast && (
   362→            <div className={`${css.toast} ${css.toastVariant[toast.kind]}`}>
   363→              {toast.kind === 'success' ? (
   364→                <Check size={12} />
   365→              ) : (
   366→                <Info size={12} />
   367→              )}
   368→              {toast.message}
   369→            </div>
   370→          )}
   371→        </div>
   372→      )}
   373→
   374→      <Dialog
   375→        open={dialogOpen}
   376→        onOpenChange={(nextOpen) => {
   377→          if (!nextOpen && dialogUploading) return
   378→          setDialogOpen(nextOpen)
   379→          if (!nextOpen) {
   380→            resetUrlState()
   381→            setDialogUploading(false)
   382→            setDialogDragActive(false)
   383→          }
   384→        }}
   385→      >
   386→        <DialogPopup
   387→          className={css.dialogPopup}
   388→          showCloseButton={!dialogUploading}
   389→        >
   390→          <div className={css.dialogHeader}>
   391→            <DialogTitle className={css.dialogTitle}>Insert image</DialogTitle>
   392→          </div>
   393→
   394→          <div className={css.dialogBody}>
   395→            <div className={css.tabWrap}>
   396→              <SegmentedControl
   397→                items={tabItems}
   398→                value={tab}
   399→                onChange={setTab}
   400→                fullWidth
   401→              />
   402→            </div>
   403→
   404→            {tab === 'upload' ? (
   405→              <div
   406→                role="button"
   407→                tabIndex={0}
   408→                className={`${css.uploadDropzone} ${css.uploadDropzoneState[dialogUploading ? 'busy' : dialogDragActive ? 'active' : 'idle']}`}
   409→                onClick={() => {
   410→                  if (!dialogUploading) fileInputRef.current?.click()
   411→                }}
   412→                onKeyDown={(event) => {
   413→                  if (event.key !== 'Enter' && event.key !== ' ') return
   414→                  event.preventDefault()
   415→                  if (!dialogUploading) fileInputRef.current?.click()
   416→                }}
   417→                onDragEnter={(event) => {
   418→                  if (hasImageData(event.dataTransfer)) {
   419→                    event.preventDefault()
   420→                    setDialogDragActive(true)
   421→                  }
   422→                }}
   423→                onDragOver={(event) => {
   424→                  if (hasImageData(event.dataTransfer)) {
   425→                    event.preventDefault()
   426→                  }
   427→                }}
   428→                onDragLeave={() => setDialogDragActive(false)}
   429→                onDrop={(event) => {
   430→                  event.preventDefault()
   431→                  setDialogDragActive(false)
   432→                  const file = Array.from(event.dataTransfer.files).find(
   433→                    isImageFile,
   434→                  )
   435→                  void handleDialogFile(file ?? null)
   436→                }}
   437→              >
   438→                <input
   439→                  ref={fileInputRef}
   440→                  className={css.hiddenInput}
   441→                  type="file"
   442→                  accept="image/*"
   443→                  onChange={(event) => {
   444→                    const file = event.currentTarget.files?.[0] ?? null
   445→                    void handleDialogFile(file)
   446→                    event.currentTarget.value = ''
   447→                  }}
   448→                />
   449→
   450→                {dialogUploading ? (
   451→                  <div className={css.uploadBusyWrap}>
   452→                    <div className={css.uploadProgress}>
   453→                      <span className={css.spinner} />
   454→                      Uploading image...
   455→                    </div>
   456→                  </div>
   457→                ) : (
   458→                  <>
   459→                    <span className={css.uploadDropIcon}>
   460→                      <Upload size={18} />
   461→                    </span>
   462→                    <span className={css.uploadDropTitle}>
   463→                      Click to upload or drag and drop
   464→                    </span>
   465→                    <span className={css.uploadDropDesc}>
   466→                      PNG, JPG, GIF, WebP
   467→                    </span>
   468→                  </>
   469→                )}
   470→              </div>
   471→            ) : (
   472→              <>
   473→                <div className={css.urlInputRow}>
   474→                  <input
   475→                    className={css.textInput}
   476→                    type="url"
   477→                    placeholder="https://example.com/image.jpg"
   478→                    value={urlInput}
   479→                    onChange={(event) => {
   480→                      setUrlInput(event.target.value)
   481→                      setUrlError(null)
   482→                      setUrlPreview(null)
   483→                      setUrlMeta(null)
   484→                    }}
   485→                    onKeyDown={(event) => {
   486→                      if (event.key === 'Enter') {
   487→                        event.preventDefault()
   488→                        if (urlPreview) {
   489→                          handleInsertByUrl()
   490→                        } else {
   491→                          void handleUrlPreview()
   492→                        }
   493→                      }
   494→                    }}
   495→                  />
   496→                  <button
   497→                    type="button"
   498→                    className={css.secondaryButton}
   499→                    disabled={urlLoading || !urlInput.trim()}
   500→                    onClick={() => void handleUrlPreview()}
   501→                  >
   502→                    {urlLoading ? 'Loading' : 'Preview'}
   503→                  </button>
   504→                  <button
   505→                    type="button"
   506→                    className={css.actionButton}
   507→                    disabled={!urlPreview}
   508→                    onClick={handleInsertByUrl}
   509→                  >
   510→                    Insert
   511→                  </button>
   512→                </div>
   513→
   514→                {urlError && (
   515→                  <span
   516→                    className={`${css.helperText} ${css.toastVariant.error}`}
   517→                  >
   518→                    <Info size={12} />
   519→                    {urlError}
   520→                  </span>
   521→                )}
   522→
   523→                {urlPreview && (
   524→                  <div className={css.urlPreview}>
   525→                    <img
   526→                      className={css.urlPreviewImage}
   527→                      src={urlPreview}
   528→                      alt="Preview"
   529→                    />
   530→                  </div>
   531→                )}
   532→              </>
   533→            )}
   534→          </div>
   535→
   536→          <div className={css.dialogFooter}>
   537→            <span className={css.helperText}>
   538→              <Link2 size={12} />
   539→              You can also paste images or drag files directly into the editor.
   540→            </span>
   541→            <div className={css.dialogFooterActions}>
   542→              <button
   543→                type="button"
   544→                className={css.secondaryButton}
   545→                onClick={() => setDialogOpen(false)}
   546→                disabled={dialogUploading}
   547→              >
   548→                Close
   549→              </button>
   550→            </div>
   551→          </div>
   552→        </DialogPopup>
   553→      </Dialog>
   554→    </>
   555→  )
   556→}
   557→
```

     1→import type { EditorConfig, LexicalEditor } from 'lexical'
     2→import { $insertNodes } from 'lexical'
     3→import type { ReactElement } from 'react'
     4→import { createElement } from 'react'
     5→
     6→import { CodeBlockEditDecorator } from '../components/decorators/CodeBlockEditDecorator'
     7→import { CodeBlockNode, type SerializedCodeBlockNode } from './CodeBlockNode'
     8→
     9→export class CodeBlockEditNode extends CodeBlockNode {
    10→  static slashMenuItems = CodeBlockNode.slashMenuItems.map((item) => ({
    11→    ...item,
    12→    onSelect: (editor: LexicalEditor) => {
    13→      editor.update(() => {
    14→        $insertNodes([new CodeBlockEditNode('', 'text')])
    15→      })
    16→    },
    17→  }))
    18→
    19→  static clone(node: CodeBlockEditNode): CodeBlockEditNode {
    20→    return new CodeBlockEditNode(node.__code, node.__language, node.__key)
    21→  }
    22→
    23→  static importJSON(
    24→    serializedNode: SerializedCodeBlockNode,
    25→  ): CodeBlockEditNode {
    26→    return new CodeBlockEditNode(serializedNode.code, serializedNode.language)
    27→  }
    28→
    29→  decorate(_editor: LexicalEditor, _config: EditorConfig): ReactElement {
    30→    return createElement(CodeBlockEditDecorator, {
    31→      nodeKey: this.__key,
    32→      code: this.__code,
    33→      language: this.__language,
    34→    })
    35→  }
    36→}

_**User (2026-03-03 16:24:14Z)**_

Base directory for this skill: <HOME>/.claude/plugins/cache/claude-plugins-official/superpowers/4.3.1/skills/executing-plans

# Executing Plans

## Overview

Load plan, review critically, execute tasks in batches, report for review between batches.

**Core principle:** Batch execution with checkpoints for architect review.

**Announce at start:** "I'm using the executing-plans skill to implement this plan."

## The Process

### Step 1: Load and Review Plan
1. Read plan file
2. Review critically - identify any questions or concerns about the plan
3. If concerns: Raise them with your human partner before starting
4. If no concerns: Create TodoWrite and proceed

### Step 2: Execute Batch
**Default: First 3 tasks**

For each task:
1. Mark as in_progress
2. Follow each step exactly (plan has bite-sized steps)
3. Run verifications as specified
4. Mark as completed

### Step 3: Report
When batch complete:
- Show what was implemented
- Show verification output
- Say: "Ready for feedback."

### Step 4: Continue
Based on feedback:
- Apply changes if needed
- Execute next batch
- Repeat until complete

### Step 5: Complete Development

After all tasks complete and verified:
- Announce: "I'm using the finishing-a-development-branch skill to complete this work."
- **REQUIRED SUB-SKILL:** Use superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch
- Follow that skill to verify tests, present options, execute choice

## When to Stop and Ask for Help

**STOP executing immediately when:**
- Hit a blocker mid-batch (missing dependency, test fails, instruction unclear)
- Plan has critical gaps preventing starting
- You don't understand an instruction
- Verification fails repeatedly

**Ask for clarification rather than guessing.**

## When to Revisit Earlier Steps

**Return to Review (Step 1) when:**
- Partner updates the plan based on your feedback
- Fundamental approach needs rethinking

**Don't force through blockers** - stop and ask.

## Remember
- Review plan critically first
- Follow plan steps exactly
- Don't skip verifications
- Reference skills when plan says to
- Between batches: just report and wait
- Stop when blocked, don't guess
- Never start implementation on main/master branch without explicit user consent

## Integration

**Required workflow skills:**
- **superpowers:using-git-worktrees** - REQUIRED: Set up isolated workspace before starting
- **superpowers:writing-plans** - Creates the plan this skill executes
- **superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch** - Complete development after all tasks


---

<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```tsx
import { $convertFromMarkdownString } from '@lexical/markdown'
import { useLexicalComposerContext } from '@lexical/react/LexicalComposerContext'
import {
  $insertNodes,
  $parseSerializedNode,
  COMMAND_PRIORITY_HIGH,
  PASTE_COMMAND,
  createEditor,
} from 'lexical'
import { useEffect } from 'react'

import { getResolvedEditNodes } from '../node-registry'
import { $createCodeBlockEditNode } from '../nodes/CodeBlockEditNode'
import { ALL_TRANSFORMERS } from '../transformers'

function getVSCodePasteData(
  clipboardData: DataTransfer,
): { language: string } | null {
  const raw = clipboardData.getData('vscode-editor-data')
  if (!raw) return null
  try {
    const data = JSON.parse(raw)
    return { language: data.mode || 'text' }
  } catch {
    return null
  }
}

function hasRichHTML(clipboardData: DataTransfer): boolean {
  const html = clipboardData.getData('text/html')
  if (!html) return false
  if (/data-vscode|vscode-/i.test(html)) return false
  const doc = new DOMParser().parseFromString(html, 'text/html')
  const richTags = doc.body.querySelectorAll(
    'strong,em,b,i,h1,h2,h3,h4,h5,h6,ul,ol,table,img,blockquote,pre>code,a[href]',
  )
  return richTags.length > 0
}

function detectMarkdown(text: string): boolean {
  let score = 0

  if (/^#{1,6}\s+\S/m.test(text)) score += 5
  if (/^\```[\w-]*$/m.test(text)) score += 5
  if (/\[.+?\]\(.+?\)/.test(text)) score += 4
  if (/!\[.*?\]\(.+?\)/.test(text)) score += 5
  if (/^\|.+\|$/m.test(text) && /^\|[-:\s|]+\|$/m.test(text)) score += 5
  if (
    /^>\s*\[!(NOTE|TIP|WARNING|CAUTION|IMPORTANT)\]/im.test(text)
  )
    score += 5
  if (/^[-*]\s+\[[ x]\]/m.test(text)) score += 4

  if (/\*\*.+?\*\*/.test(text)) score += 2
  if (/(?<!\*)\*(?!\*)(?!\s).+?(?<!\s)(?<!\*)\*(?!\*)/.test(text)) score += 1
  if (/^[-*+]\s+\S/m.test(text)) score += 1
  if (/^\d+\.\s+\S/m.test(text)) score += 1
  if (/^>\s+\S/m.test(text)) score += 1
  if (/`.+?`/.test(text)) score += 1
  if (/^[-*_]{3,}$/m.test(text)) score += 2

  const paragraphs = text.split(/\n{2,}/).filter(Boolean)
  if (paragraphs.length >= 3) score += 2

  if (text.length < 20) score -= 3
  if (!text.includes('\n')) score -= 2

  return score >= 5
}

function convertAndInsert(
  editor: import('lexical').LexicalEditor,
  markdown: string,
): void {
  const tempEditor = createEditor({
    namespace: 'markdown-paste-temp',
    nodes: getResolvedEditNodes(),
    onError: () => {},
  })

  tempEditor.update(
    () => {
      $convertFromMarkdownString(markdown, ALL_TRANSFORMERS)
    },
    { discrete: true },
  )

  const serializedChildren =
    tempEditor.getEditorState().toJSON().root.children

  const nodes = serializedChildren.map((s) => $parseSerializedNode(s))
  $insertNodes(nodes)
}

export function MarkdownPastePlugin() {
  const [editor] = useLexicalComposerContext()

  useEffect(() => {
    return editor.registerCommand(
      PASTE_COMMAND,
      (event) => {
        const clipboardData =
          'clipboardData' in event
            ? (event as ClipboardEvent).clipboardData
            : null
        if (!clipboardData) return false

        // Image files → let ImageUploadPlugin handle
        if (
          Array.from(clipboardData.files).some((f) =>
            f.type.startsWith('image/'),
          )
        )
          return false

        // VS Code paste → code block(s)
        const vscodeData = getVSCodePasteData(clipboardData)
        if (vscodeData) {
          const code = clipboardData.getData('text/plain')
          if (code) {
            const segments = code.split(/\n{3,}/).filter(Boolean)
            const nodes = segments.map((s) =>
              $createCodeBlockEditNode(s.trim(), vscodeData.language),
            )
            $insertNodes(nodes)
            event.preventDefault()
            return true
          }
        }

        // Rich HTML → let Lexical default handle
        if (hasRichHTML(clipboardData)) return false

        // Markdown detection
        const text = clipboardData.getData('text/plain')
        if (!text || !detectMarkdown(text)) return false

        event.preventDefault()
        convertAndInsert(editor, text)
        return true
      },
      COMMAND_PRIORITY_HIGH,
    )
  }, [editor])

  return null
}

```

```

   140→        return true
   141→      },
   142→      COMMAND_PRIORITY_HIGH,
   143→    )
   144→  }, [editor])

Pasting an indented Python/YAML/code fragment can become syntactically wrong.\n\n2. **High**: Markdown paste can be silently dropped on conversion/parsing failure.  \n[haklex/rich-editor/src/plugins/MarkdownPastePlugin.tsx:76](<REPO_ROOT>/haklex/rich-editor/src/plugins/MarkdownPastePlugin.tsx:76), [haklex/rich-editor/src/plugins/MarkdownPastePlugin.tsx:138](<REPO_ROOT>/haklex/rich-editor/src/plugins/MarkdownPastePlugin.tsx:138), [haklex/rich-editor/src/plugins/MarkdownPastePlugin.tsx:139](<REPO_ROOT>/haklex/rich-editor/src/plugins/MarkdownPastePlugin.tsx:139)  \n`event.preventDefault()` is called before conversion, and temp editor errors are swallowed (`onError: () => {}`). If conversion/parse fails, default paste is already blocked and user gets nothing inserted.\n\n3. **Medium**: Markdown detector is overly strict and misses common markdown inputs.  \n[haklex/rich-editor/src/plugins/MarkdownPastePlugin.tsx:44](<REPO_ROOT>/haklex/rich-editor/src/plugins/MarkdownPastePlugin.tsx:44), [haklex/rich-editor/src/plugins/MarkdownPastePlugin.tsx:66](<REPO_ROOT>/haklex/rich-editor/src/plugins/MarkdownPastePlugin.tsx:66), [haklex/rich-editor/src/plugins/MarkdownPastePlugin.tsx:67](<REPO_ROOT>/haklex/rich-editor/src/plugins/MarkdownPastePlugin.tsx:67), [haklex/rich-editor/src/plugins/MarkdownPastePlugin.tsx:69](<REPO_ROOT>/haklex/rich-editor/src/plugins/MarkdownPastePlugin.tsx:69)  \nExamples like `# Title`, short fenced blocks, simple lists, and single markdown links score below threshold and won’t convert, which is likely surprising for “markdown text paste”.\n\n4. **Medium**: VS Code segment splitting is brittle for real-world text.  \n[haklex/rich-editor/src/plugins/MarkdownPastePlugin.tsx:121](<REPO_ROOT>/haklex/rich-editor/src/plugins/MarkdownPastePlugin.tsx:121)  \nSplitting on `\\n{3,}` can incorrectly split valid code containing larger blank gaps, and it won’t split Windows `\\r\\n` multi-segment content.\n\n**Open questions / assumptions**\n1. _**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-03 16:52:55Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The Codex review found some issues. Let me address them:

1. **High: `trim()` on VS Code paste** - Valid concern. `trim()` removes leading/trailing whitespace which can corrupt indentation. I should use `trimEnd()` instead or just remove outer blank lines.

2. **High: Conversion failure drops paste** - `event.preventDefault()` is called before `convertAndInsert()`, so if conversion fails the paste is lost. I should wrap in try-catch and fall back.

3. **Medium: Markdown detector strictness** - This is by design per the plan. Single-line markdown like `# Title` alone doesn't meet threshold to avoid false positives. This is a reasonable design decision.


4. **Medium: VS Code segment splitting** - The `\r\n` handling for Windows is a fair point, and the multi-blank-line splitting is intentional per the plan. I'll focus on fixing the two high-priority issues first.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-03 16:52:56Z)**_

Codex review 发现两个 High 问题。修之。

**Issue 1**: `trim()` 会破坏缩进 → 改用去除首尾空行但保留缩进。
**Issue 2**: `event.preventDefault()` 在转换前调用，若转换失败则粘贴丢失 → 加 try-catch 兜底。

```text
     1→import { $convertFromMarkdownString } from '@lexical/markdown'
     2→import { useLexicalComposerContext } from '@lexical/react/LexicalComposerContext'
     3→import type { SerializedLexicalNode } from 'lexical'
     4→import {
     5→  $insertNodes,
     6→  $parseSerializedNode,
     7→  COMMAND_PRIORITY_HIGH,
     8→  createEditor,
     9→  PASTE_COMMAND,
    10→} from 'lexical'
    11→import { useEffect } from 'react'
    12→
    13→import { getResolvedEditNodes } from '../node-registry'
    14→import { $createCodeBlockEditNode } from '../nodes/CodeBlockEditNode'
    15→import { ALL_TRANSFORMERS } from '../transformers'
    16→
    17→function getVSCodePasteData(
    18→  clipboardData: DataTransfer,
    19→): { language: string } | null {
    20→  const raw = clipboardData.getData('vscode-editor-data')
    21→  if (!raw) return null
    22→  try {
    23→    const data = JSON.parse(raw)
    24→    return { language: data.mode || 'text' }
    25→  } catch {
    26→    return null
    27→  }
    28→}
    29→
    30→function hasRichHTML(clipboardData: DataTransfer): boolean {
    31→  const html = clipboardData.getData('text/html')
    32→  if (!html) return false
    33→  if (/data-vscode|vscode-/i.test(html)) return false
    34→  const doc = new DOMParser().parseFromString(html, 'text/html')
    35→  const richTags = doc.body.querySelectorAll(
    36→    'strong,em,b,i,h1,h2,h3,h4,h5,h6,ul,ol,table,img,blockquote,pre>code,a[href]',
    37→  )
    38→  return richTags.length > 0
    39→}
    40→
    41→function detectMarkdown(text: string): boolean {
    42→  let score = 0
    43→
    44→  if (/^#{1,6}\s+\S/m.test(text)) score += 5
    45→  if (/^```[\w-]*$/m.test(text)) score += 5
    46→  if (/\[.+?\]\(.+?\)/.test(text)) score += 4
    47→  if (/!\[.*?\]\(.+?\)/.test(text)) score += 5
    48→  if (/^\|.+\|$/m.test(text) && /^\|[-:\s|]+\|$/m.test(text)) score += 5
    49→  if (
    50→    /^>\s*\[!(?:NOTE|TIP|WARNING|CAUTION|IMPORTANT)\]/im.test(text)
    51→  )
    52→    score += 5
    53→  if (/^[-*]\s+\[[ x]\]/m.test(text)) score += 4
    54→
    55→  if (/\*\*.+?\*\*/.test(text)) score += 2
    56→  if (/(?<!\*)\*(?!\*)(?!\s).+?(?<!\s)(?<!\*)\*(?!\*)/.test(text)) score += 1
    57→  if (/^[-*+]\s+\S/m.test(text)) score += 1
    58→  if (/^\d+\.\s+\S/m.test(text)) score += 1
    59→  if (/^>\s+\S/m.test(text)) score += 1
    60→  if (/`.+?`/.test(text)) score += 1
    61→  if (/^[-*_]{3,}$/m.test(text)) score += 2
    62→
    63→  const paragraphs = text.split(/\n{2,}/).filter(Boolean)
    64→  if (paragraphs.length >= 3) score += 2
    65→
    66→  if (text.length < 20) score -= 3
    67→  if (!text.includes('\n')) score -= 2
    68→
    69→  return score >= 5
    70→}
    71→
    72→function convertAndInsert(markdown: string): void {
    73→  const tempEditor = createEditor({
    74→    namespace: 'markdown-paste-temp',
    75→    nodes: getResolvedEditNodes(),
    76→    onError: () => {},
    77→  })
    78→
    79→  tempEditor.update(
    80→    () => {
    81→      $convertFromMarkdownString(markdown, ALL_TRANSFORMERS)
    82→    },
    83→    { discrete: true },
    84→  )
    85→
    86→  const serializedChildren =
    87→    tempEditor.getEditorState().toJSON().root.children
    88→
    89→  const nodes = serializedChildren.map((s: SerializedLexicalNode) =>
    90→    $parseSerializedNode(s),
    91→  )
    92→  $insertNodes(nodes)
    93→}
    94→
    95→export function MarkdownPastePlugin() {
    96→  const [editor] = useLexicalComposerContext()
    97→
    98→  useEffect(() => {
    99→    return editor.registerCommand(
   100→      PASTE_COMMAND,
   101→      (event) => {
   102→        const clipboardData =
   103→          'clipboardData' in event
   104→            ? (event as ClipboardEvent).clipboardData
   105→            : null
   106→        if (!clipboardData) return false
   107→
   108→        // Image files → let ImageUploadPlugin handle
   109→        if (
   110→          Array.from(clipboardData.files).some((f) =>
   111→            f.type.startsWith('image/'),
   112→          )
   113→        )
   114→          return false
   115→
   116→        // VS Code paste → code block(s)
   117→        const vscodeData = getVSCodePasteData(clipboardData)
   118→        if (vscodeData) {
   119→          const code = clipboardData.getData('text/plain')
   120→          if (code) {
   121→            const segments = code.split(/\n{3,}/).filter(Boolean)
   122→            const nodes = segments.map((s) =>
   123→              $createCodeBlockEditNode(s.trim(), vscodeData.language),
   124→            )
   125→            $insertNodes(nodes)
   126→            event.preventDefault()
   127→            return true
   128→          }
   129→        }
   130→
   131→        // Rich HTML → let Lexical default handle
   132→        if (hasRichHTML(clipboardData)) return false
   133→
   134→        // Markdown detection
   135→        const text = clipboardData.getData('text/plain')
   136→        if (!text || !detectMarkdown(text)) return false
   137→
   138→        event.preventDefault()
   139→        convertAndInsert(text)
   140→        return true
   141→      },
   142→      COMMAND_PRIORITY_HIGH,
   143→    )
   144→  }, [editor])
   145→
   146→  return null
   147→}
   148→
```
</details></tool-use>

    89→  const nodes = serializedChildren.map((s: SerializedLexicalNode) =>
    90→    $parseSerializedNode(s),
    91→  )
    92→  $insertNodes(nodes)
    93→}
    94→
    95→export function MarkdownPastePlugin() {
    96→  const [editor] = useLexicalComposerContext()
    97→
    98→  useEffect(() => {
    99→    return editor.registerCommand(
   100→      PASTE_COMMAND,
   101→      (event) => {
   102→        const clipboardData =
   103→          'clipboardData' in event
   104→            ? (event as ClipboardEvent).clipboardData
   105→            : null
   106→        if (!clipboardData) return false
   107→
   108→        // Image files → let ImageUploadPlugin handle
   109→        if (
   110→          Array.from(clipboardData.files).some((f) =>
   111→            f.type.startsWith('image/'),
   112→          )
   113→        )
   114→          return false
   115→
   116→        // VS Code paste → code block(s)
   117→        const vscodeData = getVSCodePasteData(clipboardData)
   118→        if (vscodeData) {
   119→          const code = clipboardData.getData('text/plain')
   120→          if (code) {
   121→            const segments = code.split(/\r?\n{3,}/).filter(Boolean)
   122→            const nodes = segments.map((s) =>
   123→              $createCodeBlockEditNode(
   124→                s.replace(/^\s*\n/, '').replace(/\n\s*$/, ''),
   125→                vscodeData.language,
   126→              ),
   127→            )
   128→            $insertNodes(nodes)
   129→            event.preventDefault()
   130→            return true
   131→          }
   132→        }
   133→
   134→        // Rich HTML → let Lexical default handle
   135→        if (hasRichHTML(clipboardData)) return false
   136→
   137→        // Markdown detection
   138→        const text = clipboardData.getData('text/plain')
   139→        if (!text || !detectMarkdown(text)) return false
   140→
   141→        try {
   142→          event.preventDefault()
   143→          convertAndInsert(text)
   144→          return true
   145→        } catch {
   146→          return false
   147→        }
   148→      },
   149→      COMMAND_PRIORITY_HIGH,
   150→    )
   151→  }, [editor])
   152→
   153→  return null
   154→}
   155→
```
</details></tool-use>
